title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Human Genome Project was an international scientific collaboration launched in 1990 with the objective of mapping the complete sequence of human DNA, (0) which was finally achieved in 2003 after more than a decade of intensive coordinated effort. Its completion was hailed as a milestone in biological science. (9) Although the project produced a reference sequence of approximately three billion base pairs, scientists quickly recognised that understanding the genome's function would require many further decades of research. (10) Such was the complexity of the relationship between genetic sequences and observable characteristics that simplistic notions of 'the gene for' a particular trait were rapidly abandoned. The scientific community invested heavily in the necessary analytical tools, and (11) Provided that the raw sequence data was made freely available, researchers worldwide could pursue their own investigations into gene function and disease susceptibility. Not (12) only did the project generate a vast quantity of biological data, but it also stimulated the development of entirely new fields, including genomics, proteomics and bioinformatics. (13) Regardless of the speed at which data has been generated, translating genomic knowledge into clinical treatments has proved considerably more challenging than many early advocates anticipated. Not (14) until the widespread adoption of next-generation sequencing technologies did the cost of individual genomic analysis fall sufficiently to permit large-scale population studies. (15) Yet the ethical questions surrounding genomic data - including issues of consent, privacy and insurance discrimination - have not been resolved with the same efficiency as the technical challenges. These questions remain pressing for legislators and scientists alike. (16) Whether the Human Genome Project ultimately proves most transformative for medicine or for our fundamental biological self-understanding, its impact on science in the twenty-first century is already beyond question.

example.text: The Human Genome Project was an international scientific collaboration launched in 1990 with the objective of mapping the complete sequence of human DNA, (0) which was finally achieved in 2003 after more than a decade of intensive coordinated effort.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Adversative conjunction or adverb used as a discourse marker meaning 'however' or 'nevertheless', introducing a contrast after positive information.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
